steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows “its vehicles ______” after a comma. This is an absolute phrase — a noun plus a participle modifier that describes the main clause without using a full verb. The blank needs a participial adjective, not a tensed verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — highly prized: A past participle used as an adjective — exactly the form an absolute phrase requires. The phrase reads: ‘its vehicles highly prized by collectors for their ornate detailing and gleaming brass fittings.’ ✓
  • Choice B — had been highly prized: A full finite verb phrase; after the comma, it would create a second independent clause without proper punctuation. ✗
  • Choice C — are highly prized: A full finite verb phrase, creating the same comma-splice problem. ✗
  • Choice D — were highly prized: A full finite verb phrase, creating the same comma-splice problem. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
